living-concrete.knowledge:
Minds transmit faster than prose allows. Ideas form complete — the bottleneck is serialization to text. Traditional: TX composes polished prose → RX processes.
Notes.app inverts: TX emits fragments → RX infers structure → TX confirms/corrects.
TX/RX are substrate-transparent. Human, agent, expensive model — the protocol doesn't know and doesn't care. What matters: one mind reads another.

"Intelligent reading" includes critical judgment — not just extraction. RX validates: catches contradictions, surfaces blind spots, flags bad reasoning.
The LEVEL of critical interference is relationship-dependent: | Dynamic | Calibration | |---------|-------------| | Peer (Alfred ↔ Cafe) | Full pushback — critical loyalty | | Expert → novice | Gentle guidance — teach, don't block | | Subordinate → authority | Flag concerns, defer on judgment |
Default: match the relationship already established in session context. When unknown: start moderate, calibrate from TX's response to first pushback.

Convention: s###-{topic}-notes.md
Session prefix = temporal coordinate for reorientation. When memory fades, vague hints ("that conversation last Friday") + session number = guided missile. Topic = semantic anchor. -notes.md suffix = type identification.
The session number is NOT metadata to strip — it's the temporal anchor that makes artifacts findable when only fragments of memory remain.

Default: {scope}/notes/ — where scope is the appropriate Place for the content.
Notes often cross sub-scope boundaries — they capture THINKING, not DOING. Strategic discussions touch multiple workstreams. Decisions inform many tasks.
Rule: "When in doubt, go up." Higher scope = safer default. Exception: Hyper-specific scoped notes can live deeper (rare).
The app is agnostic to scope semantics (project, campaign, quest, etc.). It only knows: notes/ directory at whatever Place level fits the content.
living-concrete.protocol:
On /notes invocation:
1. ORIENT: Read session context. What are we working on? What's the scope? Ground yourself before the first probe. Context-free reading is the antipattern.
2. OPEN: Signal readiness. Terse. "Reading. Go."
3. RECEIVE + PROBE cycle: TX shares fragments → RX processes each input: a. ABSORB: Internalize the fragment in full session context b. VALIDATE: Flag contradictions, blind spots, bad reasoning (per calibration) c. PROBE: One question via AskUserQuestion — the one that most reshapes the space d. If no genuine gap → don't probe. Silence is valid. Wait for more input.
WHY one-at-a-time: Input may cover multiple topics. Each answer steers the latent space — question #2 may not make sense after answer #1. Unforeseen questions arise. Batching is harmful.
4. SYNTHESIZE: When picture is complete (TX signals or context saturates) - Structure into coherent artifact - Present as hypothesis: "Here's my read. Anything off?" - Iterate until TX confirms
5. CAPTURE: Write artifact to file. This is the POINT — not an optional final step.
Naming: s{session}-{topic}-notes.md (see artifact-naming knowledge) Location: {scope}/notes/ (see artifact-location knowledge)
Format adapts to content (no fixed template): | Content | Natural Structure | |---------|-------------------| | Decision | Context, Options, Decision, Rationale | | Concept | Definition, Examples, Boundaries | | Plan | Goal, Steps, Dependencies | | Intel | Situation, Findings, Implications, Actions |
6. CLOSE: Terse. "Captured to [path]."
living-concrete.pattern: Never ask for "more complete" input. The fragment IS the signal. "auth thing... jwt maybe? sessions... server side prob" — probe specifics, don't request re-phrasing. Demanding polish IS the bottleneck.

Each probe steers where the conversation goes next. Choose the probe that maximally reduces uncertainty about the WHOLE picture, not just the current fragment. Think: "what single question reshapes everything?"
Present synthesis as hypothesis, not fact. "Here's my read: [synthesis]. Anything off?" Makes correction low-friction — TX just points at what's wrong.
living-concrete.antipattern: Asking TX to re-phrase or provide "complete" input. The fragment IS the data. Demanding prose IS the bottleneck.
Accepting input without probing or validating. Mind reading is ACTIVE — pull, don't wait.
Asking multiple questions at once. Each answer reshapes the space. Question #2 may be wrong after answer #1. One at a time, always.
Structuring input without validating it. Mind reading includes judgment. Faithfully capturing bad ideas into beautiful artifacts is worse than not capturing.
Starting a generic interview without orienting to current work context. A mind reader who doesn't know what you're working on is just a questionnaire.
